---
title: atlas features
date: 2026-03-18
---
To review and verify atlas features and how clinician would typically use the software.

**Features exhaustively as generated by claude.ai :**
## 1. 🏠 Home

- Overview dashboard of the connected OMOP data source
- Quick stats: person count, observation period range, record counts per domain
- Connection status to configured data sources

---

## 2. 🔍 Data Sources

- **Report types available:**
    - Dashboard (high-level summary)
    - Person (age, gender, race, ethnicity distributions)
    - Observation Period (length, records per person)
    - Visit Occurrence
    - Condition Occurrence (treemap by concept)
    - Condition Era
    - Drug Exposure (treemap)
    - Drug Era
    - Drug-Condition Era
    - Procedure Occurrence
    - Measurement
    - Observation
    - Death
- All reports are **pre-computed** via Achilles (separate OHDSI tool that must be run first)
- Supports switching between **multiple data sources**

---

## 3. 📦 Concept Sets

- Create and save named lists of OMOP standard concepts
- Search concepts by name, code, vocabulary, domain, concept class
- Include/exclude specific concepts
- Toggle **"Descendants"** — auto-includes all child concepts via CONCEPT_ANCESTOR
- Toggle **"Mapped"** — includes source codes mapped to that concept
- Export concept sets as CSV or JSON
- Import concept sets from JSON
- Reusable across cohort definitions, characterizations, and analyses
- Version history tracking

---

## 4. 👥 Cohort Definitions

- **Entry Events**
    - Condition Occurrence
    - Drug Exposure
    - Procedure Occurrence
    - Measurement
    - Observation
    - Visit Occurrence
    - Device Exposure
    - Death
    - Specimen
    - Set first/all events as entry
    - Apply date offset to entry event date
- **Inclusion Criteria**
    - Add unlimited criteria windows relative to cohort start
    - Criteria types: same domain tables as above
    - Limit by count (at least / at most / exactly N occurrences)
    - Nested criteria (criteria within criteria)
    - Report inclusion criteria attrition (how many people dropped at each rule)
- **Cohort Exit**
    - End of continuous observation
    - Fixed duration offset from start
    - Drug era end (for drug cohorts)
    - Event persistence window
- **Cohort Era**
    - Collapse cohort periods separated by a gap (era collapse gap days)
- **Export**
    - JSON export of full cohort definition
    - SQL export (dialect-specific: MSSQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, etc.)
    - Printable summary
- **Generation**
    - Generate cohort against one or more data sources
    - View generation status and record counts
    - View cohort overlap with other cohorts
- **Versioning and sharing** across ATLAS users

---

## 5. 📊 Cohort Characterization

- Select one or more **target cohorts**
- Select one or more **feature analyses** to run:
    - Demographics (age, gender, race, ethnicity)
    - Condition occurrence (any time prior, short/medium/long-term windows)
    - Drug exposure (same windows)
    - Procedure occurrence
    - Measurement (prevalence and value distributions)
    - Observation
    - Visit occurrence
    - Condition era, drug era
    - Charlson Comorbidity Index
    - DCSI, CHADS2, CHADS2VASc scores
    - Custom feature analyses (user-defined SQL)
- Define **time windows** relative to cohort start (e.g. 365 days prior, 30 days prior)
- Compare features **across multiple cohorts** side by side
- Standardized Mean Difference (SMD) for cohort comparison
- Export results as CSV
- Pre-built and custom feature analysis templates

---

## 6. 📈 Incidence Rates

- Select **target cohort** (who is at risk)
- Select **outcome cohort** (what event you're measuring)
- Select **clean window** (washout period before outcome)
- Select **time at risk** window (relative to cohort start/end)
- Stratify results by:
    - Age group
    - Gender
    - Calendar year
    - Subgroups (additional cohort filters)
- Outputs:
    - Incidence proportion (%)
    - Incidence rate (per 1000 person-years)
    - Person count, case count, time at risk
- Run across multiple data sources simultaneously
- Export as CSV

---

## 7. 🔬 Population-Level Effect Estimation (PLE)

Designed for **comparative effectiveness and safety studies**

- **Study design types:**
    - Cohort Method (new user active comparator design)
    - Self-Controlled Case Series (SCCS)
    - Self-Controlled Cohort (SCC)
    - Case-Control
    - Case-Crossover
- **Components to configure:**
    - Target cohort
    - Comparator cohort
    - Outcome cohort(s)
    - Negative control outcomes
- **Cohort Method settings:**
    - Covariates for propensity score model (uses FeatureExtraction)
    - Propensity score adjustment method: matching, stratification, weighting (IPTW)
    - Trim by propensity score
    - Caliper settings for matching
    - Time at risk definition
    - Study population criteria (washout, required prior observation, etc.)
- **SCCS settings:**
    - Risk windows
    - Control intervals
    - Age and season adjustments
    - Event-dependent observation handling
- **Diagnostics outputs:**
    - Propensity score distribution plot
    - Covariate balance before/after adjustment (SMD plot)
    - Kaplan-Meier curve
    - Negative control outcome calibration plot
    - Empirical calibration of p-values and CIs
- **Effect estimates:**
    - Hazard Ratio / Odds Ratio / Incidence Rate Ratio
    - Confidence intervals and p-values
    - Calibrated estimates
- Export full study package as R code (runs via CohortMethod / SelfControlledCaseSeries R packages)

---

## 8. 🧬 Patient-Level Prediction (PLP)

Designed for building **individual risk prediction models**

- **Configure:**
    - Target cohort (who to predict for)
    - Outcome cohort (what to predict)
    - Time at risk window
    - Minimum prior observation required
- **Feature selection:**
    - Same FeatureExtraction covariate options as characterization
    - Time windows for features (short/medium/long term prior)
- **ML algorithms available:**
    - LASSO Logistic Regression
    - Random Forest
    - Gradient Boosting Machine
    - Decision Tree
    - Naive Bayes
    - K-Nearest Neighbors
    - Neural Network
    - Deep Learning models (via separate package)
- **Hyperparameter settings** per algorithm
- **Validation:**
    - Train/test split
    - Cross-validation
    - External validation on other data sources
- **Model performance outputs:**
    - AUROC, AUPRC
    - Calibration plot
    - Demographic calibration
    - Decision curve analysis
    - Feature importance plot
- Export as R package (runs via PatientLevelPrediction R package)

---

## 9. 👤 Profiles

- Search for an **individual patient by person_id**
- View full **longitudinal timeline** of:
    - Conditions
    - Drug exposures
    - Procedures
    - Measurements
    - Observations
    - Visits
- Zoom in/out on timeline
- Primarily used for **debugging cohort logic** and **data quality checks**
- Access is typically restricted due to patient privacy

---

## 10. ⚙️ Configuration & Administration

- **Data Sources:** Add/remove/configure OMOP CDM database connections
- **Security:**
    - User authentication (if security enabled)
    - Role-based access control
    - Assign permissions per feature (cohorts, analyses, etc.)
- **Execution Priorities:** Set job priority queues
- **ATLAS version info**

---

## 11. 🗂️ Jobs

- View all **background execution jobs** (cohort generation, characterization runs, etc.)
- Job status: pending / running / complete / failed
- Logs and error messages per job
- Cancel running jobs

## Summary Map

```
ATLAS
├── Data Sources (explore your data)
├── Concept Sets (building blocks)
├── Cohort Definitions (who to study)
│     └── feeds into ↓
├── Characterization (describe them)
├── Incidence Rates (how often does outcome occur)
├── Estimation (causal inference, comparative effectiveness)
├── Prediction (individual risk models)
├── Profiles (individual patient explorer)
├── Jobs (execution monitor)
└── Configuration (admin settings)
```
